tasks.name: Save the newest restaurant branding case study from 2022 or later to favorites
tasks.description: Find restaurant/food branding projects in the portfolio filtered to 2022 or later, then save the single most recent case study to your favorites. Success: exactly one restaurant/food branding case study from 2022+ is added to your favorites list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Portfolio” or “Work” link in the main navigation menu.
Use the industry filter in the portfolio sidebar and select the option labeled “Restaurant” or “Food & Beverage.”
Use the year or date filter and select only years 2022, 2023, and 2024.
Open the sort dropdown above the portfolio grid and choose the option “Newest First” or “Most Recent.”
Click on the first case study card in the filtered and sorted portfolio grid (the top-left result).
On the case study detail page, click the “Save” or heart-shaped “Add to Favorites” button for this project.
Click the “Favorites” or heart icon in the header to open the favorites list and view the saved case study.

tasks.name: Add one ecommerce and one portfolio website template under $60 with 4+ stars to cart and start checkout
tasks.description: Purchase planning: find one ecommerce and one portfolio website template, each priced under $60 and rated 4+ stars, and add both to the cart. Success: cart contains exactly 2 items (1 ecommerce template and 1 portfolio template) with individual prices under $60 and star ratings of 4 or higher, and the checkout review page is opened.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Shop” link in the main navigation, then choose the “Website Templates” category if shown.
In the category sidebar, select the subcategory filter for “Ecommerce” templates.
Set the price filter by entering “60” in the maximum price field and apply the filter.
Use the rating filter to select templates with a rating of 4 stars and above (e.g., check “4+ stars”).
Open the sort dropdown and select “Rating: High to Low,” then click on the first ecommerce template in the sorted list.
On the ecommerce template product page, click the “Add to Cart” button with quantity set to 1.
Use the breadcrumb or “Back to templates” link to return to the “Website Templates” listing and switch the subcategory filter to “Portfolio” templates while keeping the same price and rating filters.
Click on the first portfolio template in the filtered listing and click its “Add to Cart” button with quantity set to 1.
Click the cart icon in the header, then click the “Checkout” or “Proceed to Checkout” button to open the checkout review page without completing payment.

tasks.name: Request a branding package under $5000 that includes both logo design and brand guidelines
tasks.description: Compare branding service packages and choose the cheapest option under $5000 that explicitly includes both logo design and brand guidelines. Success: the project request form is submitted with that specific package selected, the budget set under $5000, and logo design/brand guidelines mentioned in the project description.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Services” link in the main navigation.
On the Services page, select the “Branding Packages” or similarly labeled section.
Scroll to the package comparison table and identify which packages list both “Logo design” and “Brand guidelines” in their feature lists.
Among the packages that include both features, note which ones have a listed price under $5000 and choose the cheapest of these options.
Click the “Start Project,” “Request Quote,” or similarly labeled button associated with the chosen package.
On the project request form, select the chosen package name from the “Package” or “Service” dropdown.
In the “Project Description” or “Brief” text area, type a short message (at least two sentences) that mentions both “logo design” and “brand guidelines.”
In the budget field, type a budget amount between $3000 and $4900 (e.g., “4500”) and select the timeline option closest to “4–6 weeks” from the timeline dropdown.
Fill in the contact fields with a name (e.g., “Jordan Lee”) and email address (e.g., “jordan.lee@example.com”), then click the “Submit” or “Send Request” button.

tasks.name: Fill the cart with 3 different merchandise items under $35 with free shipping, including at least one blue item
tasks.description: Purchase planning: select three different physical merchandise items, all under $35 with free shipping, and include at least one item offered in a blue color. Success: cart contains 3 distinct merchandise products, each priced under $35, all marked with free shipping, and at least one selected in a blue color variant.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the “Shop” link in the main navigation.
In the shop categories, click on “Merchandise,” “Prints & Merch,” or the equivalent physical products category.
Set the price filter by entering “35” as the maximum price and apply the filter.
Enable the shipping filter by selecting the “Free Shipping” option or checkbox.
Open the color filter section, select the “Blue” color option, then click the first product in the list that offers a blue color variant.
On that product page, choose “Blue” from the color options and click the “Add to Cart” button with quantity set to 1.

Return to the merchandise listing using the “Back to Merchandise” link or the breadcrumb trail, then clear the color filter so all colors are shown while keeping the price and free shipping filters active.
Sort the listing by “Price: Low to High” and click on the first two different products in the list one by one, adding each product to the cart with quantity set to 1.
Click the cart icon in the header to open the cart page and adjust the quantity of the cheapest item in the cart to “2” using the quantity selector, keeping the other two items at quantity “1.”

tasks.name: Subscribe to the monthly design tips newsletter after exploring two recent UX case studies
tasks.description: Explore recent UX case studies from 2023 or later, then subscribe to the agency’s newsletter with a focus on design tips. Success: newsletter form is submitted with frequency set to monthly, interest set to design tips, and the titles of two 2023+ UX case studies typed into the interest/details field.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Blog” or “Insights” link in the main navigation.
On the blog/insights page, use the tag or category filter to select posts labeled “UX” or “User Experience.”
Use the date or year filter to show only posts from 2023 and later.
Open the first UX post in this filtered list, scroll through it, and copy or remember its title text.
Navigate back to the filtered UX list, open the second UX post in the list, scroll through it, and copy or remember its title text.
Scroll to the bottom of the post page and click the “Newsletter,” “Subscribe,” or “Stay Updated” link or button in the footer to open the newsletter subscription form.
In the subscription form, type a name (e.g., “Taylor Kim”) and an email address (e.g., “taylor.kim@example.com”) into the corresponding fields.
Select “Design Tips” or the closest equivalent option from the “Interests” or “Topics” checkbox list and choose the “Monthly” option from the frequency dropdown.
In any optional “Notes,” “Topics you’re interested in,” or similar text area, type a short sentence that includes both UX post titles you noted, then click the “Subscribe” or “Sign Up” button.

tasks.name: Create a favorites list of 4 digital design resources under $25 from at least 3 different creators
tasks.description: Build a favorites collection of affordable digital design resources. Success: favorites list includes exactly 4 digital products, each under $25 with a rating of at least 4.5 stars, and those products come from at least 3 different creators/authors.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the “Shop” link in the main navigation.
Within the shop, select the “Digital Downloads,” “Design Resources,” or similar category that contains downloadable assets.
Set the price filter by entering “25” as the maximum price, and use the rating filter to select items rated 4.5 stars and above.
Use the category or type filter to select “Icon Sets” (or a similar subcategory), then click the first product in the filtered list.
On this product page, click the heart-shaped or “Add to Favorites” button to add this digital product to your favorites.
Return to the digital downloads listing and change the type filter from “Icon Sets” to “UI Kits” or another distinct subcategory, keeping the price and rating filters applied.
Scan the product cards and identify a product from a creator/author name that is different from the first product’s creator, then open that product and click its “Add to Favorites” button.

Return again to the digital downloads listing and select a third subcategory (for example, “Mockups” or “Illustrations”), then add two more products from creators that have not yet appeared in your favorites by opening each product’s page and using the “Add to Favorites” button.
Click the “Favorites” or heart icon in the header to open the favorites page and view the four saved digital products.

tasks.name: Plan a multi-industry app project using three mobile app design case studies
tasks.description: Review mobile app design case studies from healthcare, fintech, and education industries, then use them to fill out a project planner form. Success: project planner form is submitted with at least one insight from each of the three case studies mentioned, target launch date set to 2025-09-01, and budget range set to $50k–$75k.
steps: From the homepage, click the “Portfolio” or “Work” link in the main navigation.
In the portfolio filters, select the service type “Product Design,” “UX/UI,” or “Mobile App Design,” and restrict the year filter to show projects from 2021 to 2024.
Use the industry filter to select “Healthcare” and open the first mobile app case study that appears.
Note one key detail from this healthcare case study (for example, an outcome or feature), then use the breadcrumb or portfolio link to return to the portfolio listing.
Change the industry filter to “Fintech” and open the first mobile app case study that appears, noting one key detail from this fintech project.
Change the industry filter to “Education” and open the first mobile app case study that appears, noting one key detail from this education project.
From the main navigation or footer, click on the “Project Planner,” “Start a Project,” or similarly named page link.

On the project planner form, type a project name (e.g., “Multi-Industry Learning & Finance App”) and in the main description text area, write at least three sentences that each reference one insight from the healthcare, fintech, and education case studies you viewed.
Use the date picker or date field to set the target launch date to “2025-09-01” and select the budget range option labeled “$50,000–$75,000” or its closest equivalent.
Fill in your contact name and email fields (e.g., name: “Alex Rivera”, email: “alex.rivera@example.com”) and click the “Submit Plan” or “Send Brief” button.

tasks.name: Apply a 10% coupon to the cheapest UI kit bundle with both desktop and mobile layouts between $40 and $120
tasks.description: Find a UI kit bundle that includes both desktop and mobile layouts, costs between $40 and $120, and has a rating of at least 4 stars. Success: that bundle is added to the cart as the cheapest qualifying option and the coupon code WELCOME10 is applied in the cart.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click the “Shop” link in the main navigation.
In the shop categories, select the “UI Kits” or “UI Kit Bundles” section.
Set the minimum price filter to “40” and the maximum price filter to “120,” then apply the price filter.
Use the rating filter to select products rated 4 stars and above, then open the sort dropdown and choose “Price: Low to High.”

Starting from the top of the sorted list, click on the first product card and scan its feature list to confirm that it includes both “Desktop layouts” (or equivalent wording) and “Mobile layouts” or “Mobile screens.” If it does, treat it as your selected product; if not, return to the list and repeat this step with the next product until you find one that includes both layout types.
On the selected UI kit bundle’s product page, click the “Add to Cart” button with quantity set to 1.
Click the cart icon in the header to open the cart page.
In the cart, locate the “Promo Code” or “Coupon” input field, type “WELCOME10” into the field, and click the “Apply” or “Update” button.
After the coupon is applied, click the “Proceed to Checkout” or “Checkout” button to move to the checkout review page without completing payment.
On the checkout review page, fill in the name (e.g., “Morgan Chan”) and email (e.g., “morgan.chan@example.com”) fields and select the default or “Standard” delivery option for digital products.
